title: The One Where Chandler Can't Cry (2)

jokes.text: Oh, wait, I don't have to lie to you, you don't live here anymore. I'm eating their food.
jokes.setup: Joey starts to make an excuse for being in Monica's apartment
jokes.punchline: Realizes he doesn't need to lie since Rachel moved out, admits he's stealing food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his logic is both honest and absurd. Well-structured with clear setup/payoff.

jokes.text: Whoa, I didn't know we could date your sister.
jokes.setup: Learning Ross is dating Rachel's sister
jokes.punchline: Joey treats it like a newly discovered option for all of them
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey logic - treating dating rules like they're arbitrary group decisions. Very economical delivery.

jokes.text: Joey, we know you steal our food. I'm good.
jokes.setup: Monica calls out Joey's food stealing
jokes.punchline: Joey's casual acceptance and dismissal
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment. Joey's shamelessness is the comedy.

jokes.text: Yeah. Who should've shut their drapes is that perverted couple two doors over.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Ross and Jill closing drapes
jokes.punchline: Joey redirects to actual perverted neighbors
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - shifts from innocent to actually perverted neighbors.

jokes.text: Is that a swing? Oh, don't even ask. Yuck.
jokes.setup: Monica spotting something at the neighbors
jokes.punchline: Implied sexual apparatus and disgusted reactions
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo joke. Works but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: When Chandler made out with my sister, I was mad at him for 10 years. That was like five years ago. Yeah, you got five years left.
jokes.setup: Monica explains her anger about Chandler and her sister
jokes.punchline: Joey's threat to extend the punishment
jokes.explanation: Great callback with Joey's threatening addition. Good escalation and character consistency.

jokes.text: No, no, nothing happened. I shut the drapes to show her slides of my favorite fossils.
jokes.setup: Implication that Ross and Jill had sex
jokes.punchline: Ross's incredibly unsexy reality of fossil slides
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross moment - his nerdiness completely deflates any romantic implications. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: Something could've happened, all right? She really dug my slides.
jokes.setup: Ross defending his romantic prospects
jokes.punchline: Thinking fossil slides are genuinely seductive
jokes.explanation: Ross's delusional confidence about his slides being romantic is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Was it the 'Please don't show me another picture of a trilobite' vibe?
jokes.setup: Ross claiming Jill was giving him 'the vibe'
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sarcastic interpretation of what that vibe actually was
jokes.explanation: Excellent sarcastic comeback that specifically targets Ross's fossil obsession. Great structure and economy.

jokes.text: I cried for three days with that movie. No, wait, two. Because on the third day, my mother killed herself, so I was partly crying for that.
jokes.setup: Joey talking about crying at Bambi
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions mother's suicide as timeline correction
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark misdirection. The casual delivery of tragic information is what makes it work.

jokes.text: Yes, it was very sad when the guy stopped drawing the deer.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Bambi's mother's death being emotional
jokes.punchline: Chandler's literal, detached view of animation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler - his emotional detachment expressed through technical literalism. Excellent economy and character voice.

jokes.text: What if the puppy said, 'Help me, Chandler. All the other puppies pick on me.'
jokes.setup: Joey trying to find something to make Chandler cry
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific talking puppy scenario
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation, though the setup runs a bit long.

jokes.text: Cry? I just found a talking puppy. I'm rich.
jokes.setup: Joey's emotional puppy scenario meant to make Chandler cry
jokes.punchline: Chandler immediately sees the commercial opportunity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - Chandler's pragmatism completely derails the emotional scenario. Perfect economy and character logic.

jokes.text: That's me with the janitor, Martin. Oh, they didn't wanna come.
jokes.setup: Looking at Chandler's childhood photo from Parents' Day
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that his parents chose not to attend
jokes.explanation: Chandler's trademark deflection of childhood trauma with casual delivery. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: You're dead inside.
jokes.setup: Chandler insisting he doesn't cry and it's not a big deal
jokes.punchline: Monica's blunt diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective. Sets up the running gag that pays off later.

jokes.text: You're just saying that because you're my biggest fan.
jokes.setup: Fan complimenting Phoebe's talent
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's circular reasoning about the compliment
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe logic - sweet but nonsensical reasoning.

jokes.text: That was Phoebe Buffay, the porn star. She was in Sex Toy Story 2... Lawrence of a Labia.
jokes.setup: Mistaking Phoebe for a porn star
jokes.punchline: Porn parodies of famous movies
jokes.explanation: Standard porn parody jokes. Clever titles but not particularly original concept.

jokes.text: Well, I wouldn't call her a star, but she's really good. You should check out Inspect Her Gadget.
jokes.setup: Joey telling Gunther not to let the guy in who called Phoebe a porn star
jokes.punchline: Gunther's matter-of-fact recommendation with another porn parody title
jokes.explanation: Good Gunther moment - his deadpan delivery makes the porn knowledge funnier.

jokes.text: He's a real up-and-comer in Human Resources.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to set up Jill with Bob from work
jokes.punchline: Unintentional double entendre about 'up-and-comer'
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo. Works but is predictable.

jokes.text: Not random. Bob.
jokes.setup: Jill saying she won't date some random guy
jokes.punchline: Rachel's insistence that Bob isn't random because he has a name
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective. Rachel's determination to make Bob happen is funny.

jokes.text: I'm so stupid. I'm just this, like, incredibly pretty, stupid girl.
jokes.setup: Jill lamenting her perceived flaws
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecation that's also self-aggrandizing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - shows Jill's vanity even in self-criticism.

jokes.text: You had me doubting my fashion sense.
jokes.setup: Jill listing things Rachel made her doubt about herself
jokes.punchline: Fashion sense being as important as intelligence
jokes.explanation: Shows Jill's shallow priorities - fashion and intelligence are equally important to her.

jokes.text: Excuse me, the only thing I can't have is dairy.
jokes.setup: Rachel saying Jill wants what she can't have
jokes.punchline: Jill taking it literally about lactose intolerance
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - takes metaphorical statement literally in unexpected way.

jokes.text: All right, all right. You just blew your chances at dating Bob. Who? In Human Resources!
jokes.setup: Jill dismissing Bob
jokes.punchline: Rachel's exasperated insistence on Bob's identity and department
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the Bob running gag. Rachel's frustration makes it work.

jokes.text: I mean, who does she think she is, Princess Caroline? You're jealous of Princess Caroline? Do I have my own castle?
jokes.setup: Rachel comparing Jill to Princess Caroline
jokes.punchline: Monica taking it literally and comparing her life to royalty
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist moment but not particularly strong. Monica's literal interpretation works okay.

jokes.text: Phoebe Buffay in Buffay the Vampire Layer.
jokes.setup: Revealing the porn video featuring 'Phoebe'
jokes.punchline: Porn parody of Buffy with Phoebe's name
jokes.explanation: Standard porn parody joke. The name pun works but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: And I saw Joey was about to go in, so I ran in ahead of him... to surprise him. And then... Then I pretended that I didn't know he was in there.
jokes.setup: Explaining how he found the porn video
jokes.punchline: Chandler's elaborate lie about why he was in the porn shop
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Chandler's over-elaborate excuse reveals his embarrassment.

jokes.text: Hey, I had a terrible childhood and I don't do porn.
jokes.setup: Monica suggesting Phoebe's terrible childhood led to porn
jokes.punchline: Chandler's defensive comparison of childhoods
jokes.explanation: Great defensive Chandler moment. His logic is both dark and funny.

jokes.text: Yes, but you're dead inside.
jokes.setup: Chandler defending his normalcy despite bad childhood
jokes.punchline: Monica's matter-of-fact callback to earlier insult
jokes.explanation: Good callback delivered with perfect timing and casualness.

jokes.text: Ah. I thought I'd find you here, Nosferatool.
jokes.setup: Vampire-themed porn scene
jokes.punchline: Pun combining Nosferatu with 'tool'
jokes.explanation: Standard porn dialogue pun. Works but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Her ankle is what you're watching? Well, it's hard to tell.
jokes.setup: Joey asking about what Ross is looking at on the porn
jokes.punchline: Ross's flustered excuse about watching ankles
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward-guys-watching-porn joke. Ross's embarrassment is in character.

jokes.text: She's just doing her job. You sick bastards.
jokes.setup: Guys commenting on the porn star's movements
jokes.punchline: Ross defending the actress while calling the others perverts
jokes.explanation: Good Ross moment - his pseudo-intellectual moral stance while watching porn himself.

jokes.text: Wait, that's Ursula. That's not Phoebe. That is Ursula. Wait, wait. I can watch that. Rewind it. Rewind it.
jokes.setup: Realization that it's Ursula, not Phoebe, in the porn
jokes.punchline: Chandler immediately changing his moral stance when it's not their friend
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Chandler's flexible morality and instant attitude change.

jokes.text: What's up? Oh, my God. What am I doing?!
jokes.setup: Phoebe enters while they're watching the porn
jokes.punchline: Chandler's panicked self-realization
jokes.explanation: Standard caught-in-the-act reaction. Works but not particularly original.

jokes.text: There's no back to this couch.
jokes.setup: Monica reading self-help book
jokes.punchline: Casual observation about their furniture's inadequacy
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor. Monica's matter-of-fact delivery works.

jokes.text: Oh, that makes me feel so warm in my hollow tin chest.
jokes.setup: Monica saying she loves him despite his inability to cry
jokes.punchline: Chandler comparing himself to the Tin Man
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware reference that fits Chandler's character and the emotional theme.

jokes.text: What is wrong with you? What? You can't shed a tear for your dead wife? I mean, I left you a note from the beyond. So you didn't mean any of that? No, you robot!
jokes.setup: Monica's sweet speech about not caring if Chandler can't cry
jokes.punchline: Her complete reversal when testing his emotional response
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Monica's real feelings. The 'robot' insult is perfect.

jokes.text: You damn kids. You ring my bell one more time, I swear to... Oh, uh, Jill. Um... That's just a little game I play with the kids down the hall.
jokes.setup: Ross answering door expecting troublesome kids
jokes.punchline: Awkward cover-up when it's actually Jill
jokes.explanation: Standard embarrassing-moment humor. Ross's awkward recovery is in character.

jokes.text: Seeing some more of your super-cool slides. Wow, really? Totally. I loved them. And maybe you could finish telling me about all the different kinds of sand.
jokes.setup: Jill asking to see more of Ross's slides
jokes.punchline: Her obvious manipulation using his geological interests
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay - Jill's manipulation and Ross's gullibility work well together.

jokes.text: Yeah. I could talk them into giving you like $30. No. No way. No. And stop using my name. And shame on you. And shame on all of you. You're disgusting. Especially you, with that.
jokes.setup: Ursula offering Phoebe money to do porn
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's escalating moral condemnation ending with specific disgust
jokes.explanation: Good escalating outrage from Phoebe. The specific callout at the end adds impact.

jokes.text: So?
jokes.setup: Ross explaining that Rachel thinks Jill is using him
jokes.punchline: Jill's complete indifference to being called manipulative
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly economical. One word that perfectly captures Jill's character and confirms Rachel's suspicions.

jokes.text: You're trying to figure out where you know me from? All right, I'll give you a hint. From porn. Okay? Yeah, your pervert boyfriend watched me in a porno movie!
jokes.setup: Stranger recognizing Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's aggressive 'clarification' that embarrasses the woman's boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe solution - turning embarrassment into weapon. Aggressive but effective.

jokes.text: Oh, well, thank you for taking your tongue out of my sister's mouth to tell me that.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining he stopped kissing Jill
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sarcastic and visceral description
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic comeback with visceral imagery that expresses Rachel's disgust effectively.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, are you crying? I just don't see why those two can't work things out.
jokes.setup: Chandler finally crying after the emotional episode
jokes.punchline: He's crying about Ross and Rachel, not the intended emotional triggers
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the episode's main theme. Chandler finally cries but for an unexpected reason - character growth with a twist.

jokes.text: How gay are you?
jokes.setup: Ross rejecting Jill's advances
jokes.punchline: Jill's crude assumption about why he'd reject her
jokes.explanation: Shows Jill's shallow and offensive worldview but relies on dated humor. Very economical delivery.

jokes.text: I can't believe Jill's gone. I can't help it. I opened a gate.
jokes.setup: Chandler continuing to cry after breakthrough
jokes.punchline: His explanation that crying has become uncontrollable
jokes.explanation: Great resolution to the emotional arc. The 'gate' metaphor is perfect for Chandler's analytical nature.
